description: §6.6.9 E-REACTIVE-003 (RULING A, broadening) — the SAME error fires for a raw mutable `@var` read, not just a derived. `saveOrder()` escalates via `?{}` SQL and reads the client-side mutable `@price`; the read lowers to `_scrml_body["price"]`, which a non-CPS server-fn stub never sends, so `@price` resolves to `undefined` server-side. The pre-S250 draft scoped E-REACTIVE-003 to derived-only; it now covers ANY free client cell (the raw-@var read has the identical not-transported failure). Incidental W-PROGRAM-001 / W-SQL-ROW-UNTYPED ignored by superset.
rationale: §6.6.9: 'E-REACTIVE-003 now covers ANY free client cell read in a wholly-server function; the derived case is the sharpest instance.' A mutable `@var` read in a server fn is not transported and resolves to undefined exactly as a derived read does.
